desc:
The ground is particularly rough and stony, with small rocky outcroppings that
thrust out into the river. Only patches of short grasses are able to grow in the
soil. To the west, the River Isen flows strongly southwards. Entry into the
river is possible, although it would require climbing down from the rocks. To
the north, the rocky river bank continues with large outcroppings further away.
name: Broken, Stony River Bank

desc:
Littered with boulder-like protrusions, these plains are rather unhelpful to
travellers, but this is the only visible passage on this side of the river. The
Isen flows by in a narrow channel below here, with the bank on this side being a
sheer drop of a half-dozen yards or more. The outskirts of the Misty Mountains
are barely visible to the north.
name: Rough Riverside Basin

desc:
The rough, rocky ground here dominates the landscape for miles on this side of
the river, making travel tiresome and slow. The terrain on the other bank is
less hostile, and indeed a road is visible not far from the water. Both the
river and the road alongside it, seem to originate in the mouth of a large
mountain valley a few miles to the north.
name: Broken and Uneven River Banks

desc:
The landscape here is scarred and scattered, with countless, loose rocks and
boulders. In its stony riverbed to the west the river Isen flows, the edges are
lined with sharp rocks. The rocky terrain rises up to the south, at this point
little more than a simple slope. However, it does becomes steeper even further
south, continuing through the plains for many miles.
name: Boulder-Strewn Riverside

desc:
The water is crystal clear and deep, the stony riverbed can be seen clearly
below. The western riverbank inclines faintly to the west, at the top of which
you can see a road following the edge. The swift flow of the river runs
southwards, moving along any loose debris. High mountain walls form the eastern
bank of the river.
name: River Isen

desc:
The wide river Isen flows past at the eastern end of the valley. The water is a
bit murky here, as silt and sand deposited along its bed are turned up by the
deeper currents. A small amount of greenery clings to the low banks of the
river. The water flows on southwards, deep and strong.
name: River Isen

desc:
The river floats by, wide and deep, in a straight line from north to south. The
sandy riverbed along the bottom can be seen, through the deep and strongly
flowing water. Small ferns and young striplings have taken root here along the
riverbank, cherishing the cold water.
name: River Isen

desc:
The water is crystal clear and chill to the touch. Several stone slabs line the
sandy river bottom deep below the surface of Isen. The water flows wide and deep
here, at a gentle speed but an unforgiving force. A massive line of mountains
lies along the eastern bank, offering little foothold for both plants and
travellers.
name: River Isen

desc:
The Isen continues on southwards, the fast flowing water from the north slows
down as it approaches a great bend in the route. Rippling a soft murmur, the
fresh mountain water pass out of the valley of Isengard not far to the south.
The arms of the Misty Mountains nearly meet further south, the gap being the
only exit from Nan Curunir.
name: River Isen

desc:
The stony ground from the south ends abruptly in an outcrop of rock. The jagged
edge of the outcrop overhangs the river Isen. The river flows from the north,
past this outcrop and continues south, just west of here. Along the far bank the
paved roadway to Isengard lies, leading north alongside the river. The climb
down to the water is tricky from here; the rocks look wet and slippery. The
horizon is filled with mountain peaks, in all directions except due south.
name: Rocky Outcrop over the River

desc:
Largely brown and thinning, the grass gradually gives way to stones that form a
sharp hillside to the east. Small patches of buttercups provide the only bright
colours as far as the eye can see. The sharp and rocky banks of the River Isen
make travel to the west treacherous while to the southeast lie flat, grassy
plains.
name: Withered Grassland

desc:
The clear water cascades over a fall of stones into this azure pool, throwing
pearls of spray into the air and singing a soft murmuring song. The water is
quite cold and quite shallow, the bottom can be seen just beneath beneath the
surface, with ripples widening out from the cascade.
name: Small Lake under the Waterfall

desc:
A wide river runs through the valley, from the mountains further north. Water
rushes down in a great fury of spray and foam in a deep hollow. The cold water
is clear, but often obscured by the turbulent flow. The river calms downstream,
southwards. The west bank is rather steep and would require some effort to
scale, while the eastern mountain wall is utterly impossible to climb.
name: River Isen

desc:
The river flows steadily southwards, gently along the mountainside and the road
to Isengard, on each side of the water. The water is cold to the touch, and
fishes can be seen through the clear water. The western riverbank, up to the
road, is quite steep and the sand offers no grip.
name: River Isen

desc:
A sparkling spring, seemingly one of the few sources of water in this barren and
stony highland, drops down a series of small falls from the north and through
this small creek. South it flows through a series of large fallen boulders and
then falls over the lip of a short waterfall.
name: A Mountain Creek

desc:
The highest peak overlooking the valley, this is but a mere doorstop compared to
the mountains you can see further north. Falling away beneath your feet in a
vast semi-circle are steep cliffs and sheer overhangs. Far below you, the valley
of Nan Curunir spreads out. The Road to Isengard, only a faint and dark ribbon
on the plain, stretches on away further to the south.
name: Hazardous Mountain Peak

desc:
A dike dug deep through the plains passes through the brown dirt while bending
slightly, going from west and continuing to the northeast. The sharp edges of
the plain above create long and deep dark shadows. Together with the large
number of boulders, the area is filled with potholes and crevices. The wall to
the east is firm and steep while the southern wall is slanting slightly, with
several hand and footholds.
name: Deep Dark Crevice

desc:
Dug deep, below the plains, a dike spans the Nan Curunir from west to east. Grey
rocks and stones can be seen among the otherwise brown earth and dirt along the
walls of the dike. The northern wall is merely slanting, with several hand and
footholds it forms a convenient way in or out of the dike, while the western and
the south-eastern are sufficiently steep to prevent all scaling attempts.
name: A Dike

desc:
The dike continues along the latitude, with bare smooth walls on both sides.
With walls too high and unstructured to scale, a gap too wide for even horses to
jump, it is a formidable obstruction for any would-be invaders of the valley.
The ground of the dike is dry, with several uneven boulders lying around,
unwanted in the fields above.
name: Down a Dike

desc:
The dike is deep with smooth earth walls. Several rocks stick out through the
surface, but none large enough to use as foothold. The ground between the walls
is strewn with debris, making it hard to walk around unencumbered. The passage
continues westwards. The south wall is less steep than the others, permitting an
easy climb in and out of the trench.
name: End of a Dike

desc:
Stunted stalks of brown grass impede progress throughout the area. A small stand
of ash-leaved maple trees provides little shelter from the elements. To the
south, a barely discernible trail meanders across the lowlands while to the east
the greenish-brown prairies stretch for miles.
name: Sunken Plains beside the Foothills

desc:
Copious clover coats the plains alongside a small trail jutting to the south and
west. Several long-discarded cairns standing inches off the ground dot the
landscape alongside the road. The mighty River Isen provides the only break to
the widespread grassland dominating the area.
name: Well-trodden Pathway through the Grasslands

desc:
Stretching several leagues to the north and east, the trail lies sprinkled with
rubble from unremitting use and ill-care. Beside the road lie diminutive
collections of pebbles washed aside by downpours over time. The road slopes
gently to the south and northeast where it meets the verdant plains.
name: Meandering Path

desc:
Sharp pieces of granite and obsidian line the side of these small, craggy
foothills. The jagged stones make travel on the hillside to the east perilous. A
tiny grove of pine trees affords cover along the ridge. Flat grasslands in the
south and west provide a stark contrast to this rugged terrain.
name: Rising Steppes

desc:
Small weeds and scrubs grow between the rocks and a few scattered trees top the
hill. Between the trees, brush, and rocks, small holes lead into the ground.
Occasionally, a rabbit or two emerges from the holes that lead upward from their
burrows. A sheer granite cliff towers upwards in the north. To the east and west
the hill slopes sharply downwards while to the south lie flowing green plains.
name: Crest in the Hills

desc:
Tree-dotted slopes descend from the west and slowly give way to the easternmost
edge of a small set of foothills. Razor-sharp pieces of granite lie strewn along
the ridge, a danger while either walking or climbing. To the south, the hills
slowly give way to a soft, flowing dale punctuated by small trees, plants, and
wildlife.
name: Foreboding Hillside

desc:
Small clumps of shrunken grass gradually replace stunted scrub brush on the
slope of the descending foothills to the west. Distinguished by their sharp
edges and coarse texture, the isolated clumps of grass reveal their resilience
as they have managed somehow to take hold among the rocks at the base of the
foothills. To the south and east, the emaciated grass is slowly transformed into
luxurious fields of thick, green grass.
name: Gaunt Grass Adjoining the Ridge

desc:
Sloping gently from the rough grassland to the west, the grass sways softly when
an occasional wind blows by. Small foothills rise above the grass far to the
west while to the east lies a copse of maple and oak trees. The trees are a
conspicuously divergent change from the rolling plains that surround the rest of
the area.
name: A Depression on the Plains

desc:
This area is dominated by an algae and lilypad covered fishpond. The surface of
the brown water remains essentially calm, as only a sporadic fallen leaf breaks
the monotony of the placid water. A few maple and oak trees provide a bit of
shade most likely feeding off the moist soil surrounding the pond. The
sheltering trees and the water make this a safe haven for birds and beasts.
name: Veiled Sparkling Fishpond

desc:
Thick, golden-green grass and plants with tall stems and bright, orange flowers
intermingle on the floor of the rolling plains. The grassless prairie to the
south provides a sharp contrast to the green grass and a small group of trees
that stand atop the plains in the west. To the southeast, a diminutive trail
passes through the prairie.
name: Golden-green Grass

desc:
Erect stems hold aloft silky grey-green leaves of a featherlike shrub. Clusters
of tiny yellow flower top the end of each branch of the shrub. The omnipresent
verdant grass of the plains lies underfoot. With the exception of some seared
grassland in the southwest, the surrounding countryside appears dominated by
green meadows.
name: Abundant Shrubbery

desc:
Soft green grass coats the surface of the prairie. To the west lie additional
grasslands covered by similarly luscious meadows. Scarcely perceptible, several
leagues to the west sits tree-laiden foothills of the mountains. The trail cuts
from the southwest to the northeast here. A dense and forboding forest can be
seen far to the northeast, dominating the entire horizon.
name: Trail through a Pale Green Prairie

desc:
Near a wide stream in the east, the trail here is wet and bears deep tracks of
earlier passer-bys. Light grass grows alongside the trail with traces of horses
and other creatures feeding. Beside the trail, neatly placed, rests a trunk of a
fallen tree, which might make a good place for resting. From this vantage there
is a view of the wide plains stretching west and south and the trail that bends
northeast towards a the great forest.
name: A Bending Trail

desc:
Small bushes and stunted trees cover the banks of the stream that comes rushing
down from the north. Some ancient fishing equipment have been left lying around
on the banks, all rotten and worn out by time. The stream becomes wider and
calmer here taking a turn eastward where the faint sound of a rushing waterfall
can be heard coming from.
name: A Widening Stream

desc:
The waters streaming down from the west form a small, yet beautiful waterfall,
rushing between two large stones and spraying water around as gentle rain.
Several small brooks and streams gather at the bottom of the fall where the
stream continues its way southwards. Remains of fallen trees lie along the rocky
banks of the stream. A growth of shrubs and trees can be spotted nearby in the
east.
name: A Small Waterfall

desc:
In this grove of young trees, some of the trees have been cruelly chopped down
or torn from the ground. Heavy feet have trodden the earth here and the grass
and bushes have been trampled. The sound of a waterfall can be heard to the west
where a small stream makes its way southwards. Plains of grass stretch away far
to the horizon ahead.
name: A Growth of Trees

desc:
Seemingly endless level plains covered with tall and straight grasses can be
seen in every direction, spanning many miles to the north where the great forest
lies. To the west stands a growth of young trees and low-lying bushes. Here and
there patches of flowers grow amidst the coarse grasses trying to fight for
space and sunlight.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
For miles there is nothing but the emptiness of the plains, a yellow-green sea
of grass flowing in the wind. Blue and white flowers dot the wide expanse, and
the ground underfoot feels firm and level. Some tracks on the ground suggest
there are animals grazing on the grassy plains. Far away to the north lies the
forest of Fangorn, the shadow of its eaves looming on the horizon.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
Level grass-covered plains stretch away to the edge of sight. Tall yellow-green
grass grows like a flowing sea all around. To the south the ground abruptly
sinks into a barren, rocky tract of land that runs southwards, a grey scar
across the verdant plains of grass. Some stunted bushes cling to the edge of the
ground in the south.
name: Treeless Plains

desc:
Amidst the tall yellow-green grasses, spots of purple and blue flowers can be
seen. To the north, a solitary tree stands at the centre of a clearing. The
plains spread out in every direction in a sea of grass. Somewhere to the north
lies the great forest at the roots of the mountains. The grasslands seem to
become sparser to the east.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
Tufts of tall grass sprout from the short turf of the plains. A few shrubs and
bushes lie scattered around the grassy expanse. A low-growing tree, with smooth
bark, grows among the tufts of grass, casting a cooling shadow over a patch of
white wildflowers. The plains stretch away to the horizon all around. Far to the
north a great forest looms dark beneath a mountain range.
name: Sparse Grassland

desc:
Bunches of short grass are sparsely distributed across the level plains. Some
shrubs lie scattered among the grasses. In places the short turf has withdrawn
and bare soil has been exposed. A wide field of tall golden-yellow hay lies to
the south. The grassy plains spread out to the north towards the great forest
that lies at the root of the mountains.
name: Sparse Grassland

desc:
Tall golden-yellow hay sways back and forth when a gust of wind blows from the
plains. The flowing fields of hay spread out to the east while elsewhere the
level grasslands stretch away to the horizon. A stunted tree, with shaggy bark,
stands on a grass-covered isle amidst the waves of golden hay.
name: A Grassy Isle

desc:
Tufts of taller grass sprout from the short turf that covers the plains. Some
scattered low trees and shrubs grow on the wind-beaten grassland. In some spots
the grass breaks away to expose the barren soil. To the north and east lie wide
flowing fields of golden-yellow hay, swaying when the wind blows across the
plains.
name: Sparse Grassland

desc:
Clusters of grass sway silently in the wind, thrusting out from the dry land.
Tracks of horses and other animals can be seen on the short turf of the plains.
In the west a rocky tract of land slopes steeply northwards. The sparse grasses
spread out elsewhere as far as the eye can see. The dry land has cracked in
places from the lack of water.
name: Sparse Grasses

desc:
Tufts of taller grass thrust out from the short turf of the dry land, the hard
ground cracking here and there from the lack of water. To the west the land
suddenly falls down to a rocky strip of sunken land. In the east a broad field
of high hay sways in the wind. The level plains stretch away to the south.
name: Sparse Grassland

desc:
The ground gradually becomes harder and the turf shorter. White flowers grow
amidst the sparse grasses, their faint scent sweet in the air. To the west the
land abruptly sinks into a rocky cleft, a deep gash in the grassy plains.
Protruding rocks and jagged stones make it difficult to climb down the incline.
The plains spread out in every direction.
name: Sparse Grassland

desc:
The rocky tract of land here has sunken some fifteen feet below the level of the
grassy plains. Piles of rocks and pebbles litter the bony ground, while
boulders, some the size of a man, makes it difficult to walk here. The walls are
steep and rugged, with sharp jagged stones protruding from them. This strip of
land stretches to the south where it will gradually climb up to the surrounding
plains.
name: A Strip of Sunken Land

desc:
The strip of land running here from the north has sunk below the surrounding
area, its rocky floor some fifteen feet lower than the grasslands around. To the
south the ground gradually begins its climb to the plains above. The walls of
the fissure are steep and jagged with stones. The floor of the cleft is littered
with stones and pebbles.
name: A Strip of Sunken Land

desc:
To the north the land slopes steeply down into a deep rocky fissure, a gash in
the surrounding verdant plains. Only a single stunted, thorny bush grows on the
stone-strewn slope. Sharp rocks thrust out from the bony ground where the dry
land has cracked. Short, dry grass grows on the edges of the slope.
name: A Rocky Slope

desc:
A stream runs across the plains to the west, its waters shimmering in the
background of the yellow-green fields. In the east, the land starts to slope
down towards a rocky strip of sunken land that runs from north to south. The
grasses grow tall and coarse, sometimes reaching two feet in height. The plains
stretch away to the south.
name: Uncultivated Fields

desc:
The green plains stretch away to the edge of sight. Tall and coarse grass grows
in every direction in a yellow-green sea dotted with blue and white flowers. To
the east the ground sinks down to a rocky strip of land running from north to
south, an ugly scar in the green plains.
name: Uncultivated Fields

desc:
Yellow-green grasses, occasionally reaching as high as three feet, cover the
plains. A pile of rocks sits nearby in the east where the land abruptly sinks
down to a rocky tract of land, running from the north to the south. Some tangled
weeds hug the sides of the rocky pile and a few flowers of blue and white can be
seen amidst the tall grass.
name: Treeless Plains

desc:
Nothing but the vast emptiness of the grassy plains can be seen. Seemingly
endless flowing fields of tall and coarse grass continue in all directions.
Spots of blue and white flowers dot the sea of grass. The ground underfoot feels
firm and even. Far to the north the Misty Mountains rise and at their feet the
eaves of the great forest loom.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The treeless, level grass-covered fields expand far to the horizon ahead. Some
low-lying bushes peek through the tall grass, surrounded by spots of blue and
white flowers. A hole, apparently a burrow of a fox or some other small animal
can be made out near the bushes. A stream cuts southwards across the grasslands
in the west like a silver thread on a green floor.
name: Treeless Plains

desc:
The stream here is narrow and ever swifter as it bends sharply southwards across
the grassy plains towards the awaiting marshlands. The banks of the stream are
deep and bare of any vegetation. A man sized boulder exposed by the constantly
flowing waters sits near one of the banks. The emptiness of the broad plains of
grass expand far beyond the stream.
name: A Narrow Stream

desc:
Wobbly cobblestones lie atop the surface of the trail. Fertile steppes border
the road to the north and east, sprinkled by small groups of trees and assorted
flora. The road runs west and south through the plains for several miles,
interrupted only by irregular, erosion-induced blemishes.
name: Twisting Trail through the Plains

desc:
Lengthy, luxuriant grass covers a thick, springy soil throughout the surrounding
locale. Underneath the thick grass, the rich earth provides an extremely fertile
area for a wide range of flora to prosper. A long, treeless slope to the north
rises above the gently sloping lands while to the southwest an unadorned trail
juts across the flat grasslands.
name: Gently Sloping Flatlands

desc:
Short stalks of green grass shift underfoot throughout the area. Aside from a
few rocky crags and the occasional animal hole, the soft grassland allows for
easy travel. With the exception of a trail to the south, golden-green grass
covers the plains as far as the eye can see in all directions.
name: Green Flowing Grassland

desc:
Beautiful, lush grass abounds as far as the eye can see. Amalgamated among the
grass stands waist high plants with lance-shaped, hairy leaves and pink, tubular
flowers measuring a few inches in length. A scarcely distinguishable trail mars
the grassland to the southwest while an effervescent meadow of clover sits atop
the land to the south.
name: Pleasant Grassland

desc:
Numerous rocks poke their heads out of the short and rough grass ubiquitous
throughout this area. Some of the boulders appear bunched together in small
piles. Long, thin blades of grass grow between the clusters of rocks. Beautiful
grasslands surround most of the area. In the south and east, bumpy trails made
of dirt cut across the grassland heading in opposite directions.
name: Rough Grassland near the Trails

desc:
Cutting east and south, the dirt trail stands out atop the green grass of the
prairie. Small holes caused by dislodged rocks mark the earthen surface of the
roadway and make travel tricky. Small rocks have collected beside the trail,
probably a result of getting washed aside by rain. Past the trail in the
northwest and southeast sits bountiful green grassland.
name: Rough Trail

desc:
Characterized by long, deep ruts and small, crusty holes, the trail roughly runs
a north to west path across the rolling meads. Portions of the road remain
firmly compacted, showing that at one time the road was a popular thoroughfare.
Green countryside stretches before one's eyes for miles in both the southeast
and northwest.
name: Well-trodden Trail

desc:
Profuse hordes of shrubs of all sizes top the grass-covered countryside. Many of
the low-growing, evergreen shrubs posses leaves with a leathery feel, oval in
shape with tapering ends. Some taller plants with serrated, yellowish leaves
have an unforgettable scent and lucid veins. Various types of foliage, primarily
shrubs and grasses, cover the surrounding countryside. The only noticeable
exception is a trail on the northwestern horizon.
name: Unstinted Undergrowth

desc:
Thick brush makes travel difficult. Many of the plants stand several feet taller
than the profuse grass. The toothed spines of one plant cut at any weak surface.
The bright rose-coloured flowers clustered at the top of another thick-stemmed
plant release an extremely powerful scent. To the south, the vegetation thins
considerably as rocky tips peek through the grass.
name: Dense Brush

desc:
Thick and tall grasses, coloured in shades of green, cover the plains. The grass
grows here at different heights making these plains look uneven. The ground is
moist and fertile with occasional puddles of water forming in small dips and
furrows. The verdant grasslands stretch away, all the way to the feet of the
Misty Mountains rising in the northwest.
name: Green Open Plains

desc:
A large untended patch of crops has survived the nature and grows here wildly,
golden rows of wheat rustling in the occasional breezes. Remains of a fallen
fence lie rotting on the ground beside the field. There are no remains of tools
or signs of recent farming. Apparently, the farm has been abandoned long ago. A
barely distinguishable path makes its way northwards. To the east lies a
farmyard.
name: An Abandoned Farm

desc:
From this higher tract of land the stream below can be seen, making its way
steadily southwards. The stream is forced to take a slight turn to the east
where it meets the wide grassy mound. In the west stands a farm, judging by its
decayed state it is apparently abandoned. A ramshackle wooden structure stands
just to the west. The grassy plains continue far to the southern horizon.
name: Grassy Hill Overlooking a Stream

desc:
The stream flowing in from the north narrows here considerably, bending east
where it continues its meandering way across the grassy plains southwards
towards the marshes that lie ahead. A thin veil of mist floats above the sodden
fields to the south. A grassy hillock overlooks the stream in the west. The
climb up should not be too difficult.
name: A Narrowing Stream

desc:
The relatively swiftly flowing stream coming from the west cleaves deeply into
the grassy plains which stretch themselves for many miles around. The stream
makes a sharp turn southwards towards the marshlands that loom still far ahead.
The banks of the stream are bare of any vegetation and should be easy to climb.
name: A Bend in the Stream

desc:
The stream here is stronger and swifter and its banks deep cloven. The waters
rushing in from the north create enough turbulence to bring up the basic
sediment making the stream muddy and filled with clods. A thick row of bushes
grow on the western bank, making passage there impossible. The stream continues
its way east.
name: A Deepening Waterway

desc:
A small brook with brownish water carefully makes its way east where it joins
another stream. The ground is wet and muddy near the brook. A large patch of
thorny bushes grow huddled together near the stream in the east. They are low
grown but dense and with their combined width they make the passage east
difficult. A barely distinguishable path leads south towards an apparently
abandoned farm.
name: A Patch of Bushes

desc:
Only few small trees and bushes reach their branches above the tall green grass.
The ground slopes slightly downwards in the west where the grasslands turn more
marshy. In all other directions, the grass-covered plains expand as far as the
eye can see. Far to the north lies the great forest and to the northwest are the
skirts of the Misty Mountains.
name: Green Open Plains

desc:
Lower than most of the surrounding countryside, the ground appears as though it
collects a lot of rain water making this area feel quite humid. Numerous
cattails grow around the edges of the more water-filled ground, evidence of just
how flooded this area can become. A dense, brown furry spike tops the tall stems
and light green, flat leaves of the cattails. A small trail snakes across the
plains to the north while thick brush dominates the landscape to the south.
name: Humid Plains

desc:
The trail crosses the thick grassland, heading north and west across the plains.
Pockets of vegetation grow in areas where the surface of the road has weakened.
Beside the road to the east and south stand shrubs of many sizes and varieties
mixed among the endless fields of grass. To the north, the road continues to cut
a path across the undulating prairie.
name: Twisting Path across the Plains

desc:
Zigzagging slowly across the prairie, the trail continues a roughly southwest to
northeast jaunt. An occasional small bush and even a short tree can be
sporadically seen on both sides of the trail. Scorched terrain to the north
presents a vivid difference to the flowing, grassy fields in the southwest.
name: Winding Trail

desc:
Tall milkweed plants spring up among the thick grass. Strong, thick stems allow
the long, rough-textured seed pods of the milkweed to hover above the tips of
the yellow and green blades of grass. The height of the milkweed becomes even
more impressive when considering the short, rough grassland to the north and the
flat trail in the south.
name: Milkweed among Arid Fields

desc:
Flourishing grasses predominantly coat the plains here. The grass appears very
similar to the north and east but in the north it is interspersed by masses of
wild flowers. On the southeastern horizon, a trail slices a diagonal path across
the plains.
name: Thriving Prairie beside the Wildflowers

desc:
Standing nearly two feet high in some areas, tall stalks of yellow-green grass
occasionally sway gently. The earth beneath this grass seems more fertile than
the surrounding countryside but what could have caused this is unknown. The
grassland continues on in every direction except the east where bountiful
quantities of exquisite wildflowers dominate the landscape.
name: Tall Fertile Grass

desc:
The fertile grassland underfoot continues unabated in every direction. Several
small conifer trees grow on the prairie and provide minimal shelter from the
elements. The only discernible breaks to the abundant grasslands are a few small
foothills in the north and a small trail running across the southern and eastern
horizons.
name: Among Thriving Grasses

desc:
Thick snarls of thistles cover the grasslands everywhere. Standing nearly two
feet high, the thistles are marked by sharp barbs and small, purple flowers. Far
to the north lie several small foothills, while to the southwest runs a small
road. Vast, undulating plains dominate the remaining landscape.
name: Tangled Thistles on the Prairie

desc:
Dense, yellow-green grass covers most of the plains with the exception of a
trail perceptible to both the west and south. The edges of stones thrust from
among the grass and make travel tiresome. Sprouts of clover rise amidst the
grass and create a thick tangle, which serves to further hinder travel.
name: Atop the Rolling Prairie

desc:
The stone-strewn land to the north slowly gives way to a lush, grassy plain
spotted by several copses of hickory trees as well as plentiful clusters of
cowslip. Similarly sculpted grasslands extend to the east and south. Far to the
west, the fast-moving waters of the River Isen are barely discernible.
name: Jagged Plains beside the Foothills

desc:
Small boulders and tiny stones lie strewn atop the grass from a recent rockslide
down the hills to the north. Tufts of grass poke out from among the stones. The
rocks slowly give way to tough, green grass that stretches far to the south and
east. A small trail is barely discernible to the southwest.
name: Emaciated Grassland along the Rise

desc:
Tiny mounds gradually give way to foothills in the north. Numerous boulders and
smaller stones mar the thinning grass. Small patches of vegetation also gash the
withered grass in places, leaving large clumps of soil uncovered. To the
northwest lies the peak of the small foothills while to the south and west lie
smooth, flourishing grasslands.
name: Amid the Tapering Knolls

desc:
Buttercups and foxglove grow abundantly here, a stark contrast to the coarse and
stony grassland to the north. Vibrant flowers can be seen in all around. Lesser
foothills to the northwest and the craggy plains to the north represent the only
break to the predominant sea of grass throughout the area.
name: Surrounded by Wildflowers

desc:
Abundant flowers grow unrestrained. Flowers with yellow centres and sun-kissed
petals represent the most copious variety. To the west, additional clusters of
untamed plant life sits atop the green prairies. In the east the beautiful
flowers end abruptly and give way to dry earth possessing little vegetation; a
vivid contrast to the rolling plains prevalent in the south.
name: Bountiful Wildflowers

desc:
Rough weed grass represents the only form of vegetation in this dry, forsaken
plain. Small cracks in the earth reveal a dire lack of water and the cause of
the lack of plant life. The area to the north and west provide a stark contrast
to the arid conditions here as flora and even a few trees can be seen. To the
south and east, the desiccated landscape continues.
name: Craggy Fields atop the Plains

desc:
Marked by thinning grass and large blotches of bare earth, the ground appears
dry and plagued by cracks. Although little vegetation grows here, few stones mar
the landscape. A close look at the scorched earth divulges the tracks of the
small animals that inhabit the plains. In all directions, grass grows more
profusely but remains disfigured by stones and other markings.
name: Parched Earth

desc:
Leading away to both the east and south, a small trail splits the grassy meadows
in two. A large number of rocks and stones lie strewn atop the roadway, most
likely ripped from the roadway by some traveller. Thick, plush grass covers the
land on both sides of the road. The grassland to the north appears particularly
vibrant.
name: Rocky Trail

desc:
From the west, the trail winds slowly across the rolling meads. Large, deep ruts
mar the road's ragged surface in places, adding to the danger created by other
holes. The trail narrows considerably to the northeast as it cuts between a
river to the east and a forest to the northeast. To the south, a large number of
cattails stands beside a peaty bog. In the north, lush grassland covers the
floor of the vale.
name: Snaking Trail through the Plains

desc:
Short and trampled straws lay between the patches of green grass. Some sleek and
tender bushes stretch their branches a few feet above the ground. The light
brown soil is damp and fertile with puddles of water forming here and there. A
trail can be seen nearby in the west.
name: Off the Trail

desc:
A few streaks of grass and small bushes mark the edges of the sodden ground. The
soil is brown and muddy forming an intricate pattern of dark brown and green
where the grass starts to grow again. A small stream can be seen making its way
southwards in the east. The faint sound of a waterfall can be heard at some
distance in the north. The plains stretch away far to the southern and western
horizons.
name: Muddy Plains

desc:
The stream slowly grows wider and easier to navigate along its southward course.
A couple of bushes grow on the grassy banks of the waterway. The faint sound of
rushing water can be heard coming from the north where the current seems to get
somewhat stronger. The water here is too muddy and deep to make out the bottom
of the stream.
name: A Wide Waterway

desc:
Coarse yellow-green grasses, about two feet high, cover the plains. A grove of
young trees lies to the north, where the faint sound of a waterfall can be heard
coming from. A stream running southwards can be seen cutting across the plains
in the west where it bends this way, passing nearby in the south.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The view from the top of this high, dead tree is marvellous. The valley floor
stretches out in all directions. The view is only limited by the dust clouds
that lie over the dry, dusty plains. The branches of the tree are sturdy and
have formed almost a platform up in the crown. Several birds are perched up on
the high branches, spying for prey.
name: Atop the Dead Tree

desc:
The yellow-green grass completely dominates the area. Not a single flower, rock,
or weed can be seen anywhere in this sea of grass. The grass is a uniform two
feet tall, and every strand stands on end. To the north and east the ground
looks black in places, suggesting a fire might have raged and scorched the earth
there.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The grasses have formed a circular clearing in the middle of the plains. At the
centre of the clearing stands a solitary tree. The tree has big leafy branches
and looks like it would be a good climb. The clearing is dotted with red flowers
and a few animal tracks can be seen leading away from the tree. The grass
continues in all directions.
name: A Clearing in the Plains

desc:
The yellow-green grass completely dominates this area. There is no sign of any
other flora having ever been here. The grass is very thick, making walking
difficult, but not impossible. Some animal tracks are seen heading into the
clearing to the east. The scent of grass overpowers all other smells.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The plains are covered in yellow-green grasses, with the tallest reaching about
two feet in height. Every once in a while there is a small flower or weed, about
to be choked out by the grass. Some animal tracks wind in between the blades of
grass. The plains are all that can be seen.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The grass in this area has taken on a peculiar brownish tint. It is just as tall
and straight as it is everywhere else. Small clumps of flowers are pushing their
way through the thick grass, attempting to reach any available sunlight. Small
animal tracks are running all over this area. The grass continues in all
directions.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
A multitude of flowers dot this area of the plains. The colour of the flowers is
almost drowned out by the grasses filling the plains. They stand straight, even
during the hardest wind. Around the flowers, there are some tracks from small
animals leading off into the grass.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The plains are full of a yellow-green grass. The grass is standing tall, like it
is guarding something important. Small flowers are dotting the landscape, adding
to the overall colour of the area. A stream can be seen running to the west,
with what appears to be a small footbridge allowing passage across it. The
grasses continue on into the eastern horizon.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
A rushing stream continues down from the forest to the west. A dilapidated
wooden foot bridge spans a shallow place along the stream's course. Just past
the bridge, the stream curves sharply south, becoming the main tributary to a
small lake. A trail leads north into the plains from the foot of the bridge.
name: A Dilapidated Stream Crossing

desc:
The scent of rotting trees hangs in the air. Many of the trees seem to have been
torn out of the ground and their trunks litter the area. The scattered remaining
trees would provide little shelter from the sunlight. There are small amounts of
grass and what look like weeds poking out from underneath a layer of tree bark
that covers the ground. A small stream flows down to the east.
name: Small Forest

desc:
The layer of bark seems to dwindle in this area. More of the grass is visible
and it is able to stand up to its full height. The number of trees is getting
smaller and smaller until the forest finally gives way into a field of two-foot
tall grasses.
name: Edge of a Small Forest

desc:
Grass has completely dominated the plant life in this area. Not a single flower
or weed seems to have survived the squelching effect of the grasses. The grass
stands up straight like the hair on a cold person's arm. To the west, the
southern edge of the Misty Mountains rises into the sky. Off to the south lies a
ragged little forest. The back of a camp blocks travel east.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The trail continues from the east on its way south. A few shaggy looking trees
surround the edges of the path, offering some shade from the sun during the day.
The trail is covered with grass and weeds, suggesting not many people have
travelled here recently.
name: A Trail on the Edge of a Forest

desc:
The trail continues from the north, heading west towards a small forested area.
The trail is barely visible here, being almost completely covered by weeds and
grasses. Endless fields of grass extend to the east and south, but there is a
glimpse of some water off to the south.
name: A Trail through the Plains

desc:
The yellow-green grasses here are about two feet tall. A few small weeds have
taken hold around what looks like a foxes burrow. The grasses, not being
obstructed by anything, are standing tall. Looking east, a field of blue flowers
can be seen. To the north and west lies a small, rarely travelled trail.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
A small, bluish flower has taken hold in this area. There are so many of them,
that the grass is actually being choked out by the flowers. The grass is still
populating a small area, but it is slowly being covered up by the flowers. The
flowers stretch to the north, but don't seem to have spread elsewhere.
name: Field of Flowers

desc:
Small red flowers dot this area, poking up through the grasses. A few blue
flowers are scattered on the western edge of the area, attempting to rejoin
their companions. The grasses stand tall, running off of the hillock to the
north. A small patch of weeds is growing under a rock. More grasses await to the
east and south.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
There are some rather large patches of red flowers surrounded by the grass. A
few orange flowers have taken up residence in the centre of the red flowers.
Mosses and weeds grow in the shadow of a large rock that was deposited here some
time ago. The grasses stretch out in all directions.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The yellow-green grasses stand up to two feet tall. They are stiff, even in the
strongest wind. There are red flowers dotting the landscape, along with some
weeds that are growing under the shade of some rocks. A clearing lies to the
south, with a tree in the centre. The grasses extend in all other directions.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The outer edge of a fire burned away some of the eastern area of the grass. Many
red flowers can be seen sprouting out of the charred black ground. Some grass
has also started to grow back in the burnt soil. The ground slopes down to the
north, leading into a dip in the plains. The grass continues to the south and
the west.
name: Regrowing Plains

desc:
The land slopes down into a bowl filled with yellow flowers. The grasses grow
down the sides, stopping once they reach the bottom of the bowl. The border of
Fangorn Forest lies up the slope to the north. Tall, yellow-green grasses grow
to the east and west, but the ground looks blackened to the south.
name: A Dip in the Plains

desc:
The grass stands tall at two feet high. Small red flowers break up the
yellow-green monotony. Weeds are growing wherever they can survive. The ground
slopes to the east resulting in a dip in the plains. The border of Fangorn
Forest stands to the north while grasses extend to the west and south.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The grasses stand tall, as if they had been marching towards the border of the
forest to the north but then been abruptly called to a halt. Some weeds grow
among a few scattered rocks. A small hillock rises out of the plains to the
west. The grasses extend to the east and to the south.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
Yellow-green grasses spread all over this small hillock. A few red flowers and a
small tree are fighting for space within the dense grass. At the base of the
hillock lies the entrance to a large den. From the top most peak of the land the
surrounding areas become visible.
name: A Hillock in the Plains

desc:
The trail disappears into a massive bed of blue flowers. The flowers seem to
stop just past the north side of the trail, west of where it turns sharply
north. The grass is almost at war with the flowers for control of the trail. The
edge of the Fangorn Forest looms to the north.
name: Trail Covered by Flowers

desc:
The trail, the part not covered by the grass, is running from east to west. The
rest has been completely enveloped by the wild grasses. The forest of Fangorn
can be seen to the north and the endless plains of grass reach out to the south
and east.
name: A Trail through the Plains

desc:
The trail continues from the east, cutting off a small section of plains to the
north. The trail is covered in weeds, unobstructed by the passage of people.
Time has not been good for this trail, as most of it is being slowly covered by
the wild grasses. The trail continues on southward.
name: A Trail through the Plains

desc:
Massive rowan trees stand here, seemingly untouched by the passage of time. The
trees are covered with dead and decayed leaves and the forest floor is very
rocky. Though not very far into the forest, the amount of light during the day
has already vastly decreased. The air seems thicker in here, making it harder to
breath.
name: Under the Eaves of the Entwood

desc:
The ground slopes upward as the plains approach the Misty Mountains. The grasses
begin to dwindle and are being replaced by rocks and other debris. Fangorn
Forest lies off to the east. To the south, you can see fields of wild grasses
with a small green dot far away. To the north, the peaks of the Misty Mountains
stand tall.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
The rocky terrain slopes slightly upwards towards the north. The forest has the
smell of stale leaves. In a few places, yellow rays of daytime sun pierce
through the forest canopy. The forest is very dim with pine and fir trees all
around. There are no signs of animals ever living here as there are no plants
for them to eat.
name: The Border of the Forest

desc:
A vertical wall of stone rises to the north, dotted with deep-green plants.
Large amounts of debris litter the ground around the base of the wall. One area
of the rock face seems have a larger concentration of plants then the rest of
the area. The wall extends to the west and slopes down slightly to the east.
name: Beneath the Ridge

desc:
The base of the mountain is covered with rocks of all shapes and sizes. Small,
green shrubs cling to the rock. The slope of the mountain to the north is not
very steep, allowing even the most inexperienced to scale it easily. The
mountain rises to the north, allowing for a good view of the surrounding areas.
The grass stretches out to the south, dotted with some colour from the flowers.
name: Base of a Mountain

desc:
A vertical wall of stone rises to the north, dotted with deep-green plants.
Large amounts of debris litter the ground around the base of the wall. Small
green shrubs grow in patches on the ground. The wall extends to the east and up
the mountain.
name: Beneath a Ridge

desc:
With Fangorn Forest so close to the north, it is no wonder why this field has
not had much traffic. The grasses have grown to about four feet high in this
area, and with nothing to hold them back, they could grow even taller. The
grasses continue on into the east, and a trail can be seen to the south.
name: A Field near the Border of Fangorn

desc:
This field has not seen much traffic in a long while. The grasses have reached a
height of four feet and stand straight on end. The air seems to be a bit thicker
here than in the surrounding areas. A disused trail can be seen to the south
while more of the tall grasses grow to the west.
name: A Field under the Eaves of Fangorn

desc:
The trail runs up to the edge of Fangorn and disappears into the trees. The
grasses running along the trail break off and begin to taper as the trail enters
Fangorn. A few solitary flowers have taken hold along the edge of the trail. The
trail continues on to the south, were it is swallowed up in a field of blue. The
air seems a bit thicker in this area.
name: A Trail out of the Forest

desc:
These high-reaching trees stand unwavering, marking what is the entrance to the
forest for some and a step closer to the fresh air plains just outside the
forest boundary to the south. Regardless of the direction being travelled, it is
difficult to ignore the stark contrast of the airy plains against the dank
confinement of the forest which is thicker to the north.
name: The Forest Boundary

desc:
The trees are covered in lichen and dead leaves, showing their ages and through
the number of seasons they have witnessed. The air is thick and dank from being
trapped between the moist ground and the canopy of limbs above. East and west
the border of the forest continues displaying a very clear natural boundary
between the two areas.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
The yellow-green grasses that approach the outer borders of Fangorn Come to an
abrupt stop at the forest's boundary. The lack of light, because of the thick
canopy of tree limbs and the musty smell of trapped air and moisture is a stark
contrast to the plains just a short walk to the south.
name: The Forest Border

desc:
The air is thick here, trapped between the moist moss and grass covered soil and
the large canopy of limbs that cover much of the forest. The air has an almost
stale quality about it, unlike the fresh airs that fill the grasslands to the
south. Toward the north the air and the trees both grow thicker.
name: The Forest Boundary

desc:
As the grasses approach the edge of Fangorn, they seem to shrink smaller. The
grass ends about a foot before the forest border. The dark pine trees stand
tall, like they are guarding the forest border. The air is stale and thick here,
making it difficult to breathe.
name: The Forest Edge

desc:
The grass is overwhelmed by the massive trees of Fangorn. The trees seem as if
they have little trouble asserting their dominion over all other plantlife. Even
the air within the forest is under the tree's command, trapped between their
tangled roots in the moist ground and the thick canopy of limbs above. Very
little fresh air seems to reach this place through the holes in the forest
canopy let in an equally scant amount of the light.
name: Dwindling Forest

desc:
The grasses shrivel as they approach the forest. The birch trees appear to be
guarding the edge of the forest from intruders. A short, green grass is growing
in patches around the bases of the trees. In a few places, what looks like the
remains of a trail can be seen. The trail appears to lead south, out of the
forest. The bulk of Fangorn lies to the north.
name: The Edge of the Forest

desc:
A warm, green grass covers the entire area. Some sweet smelling ferns grow
between the roots of some of the larger birch trees. The trees are clad in the
leaves of a generation past, while one of the trees is covered only in lichen.
What looks like plains can be seen through the trees to the south.
name: Inside the Forest

desc:
The ground slopes down from the north and then flattens out. Many rowan trees
are growing here, covered with dead leaves. Grass grows out from between the
tree roots and a few ferns have taken hold at the feet of the larger trees. The
plains can barely be seen through the trees to the south.
name: A Group of Rowans

desc:
The ground levels out as it comes down from the cliff to the north. A few birch
trees are growing here, covered with dead leaves. The roots of the trees wander
around below ground before emerging on the surface, only to dive back
underground. Warm, green grass grows all over the area. The plains can barely be
seen through the trees to the south.
name: Inside the Forest

desc:
A lot of rowan trees are growing here, their roots dominating the forest floor.
The trees are covered with old, dead leaves. Grass grows anywhere it can find
the space to grow. None of the ferns seen elsewhere in the forest has been able
to grow here. The plains can be faintly seen through the trees to the south. The
forest extends in all directions.
name: A Group of Rowans

desc:
The rowan trees continue to grow as the forest floor increases sharply in
altitude. The ground is almost completely free of tree roots, allowing the green
grasses and ferns to flourish. The branches of the trees create a barrier so
thick, not even the daytime sunlight can penetrate to the forest below.
name: A Track of Rowans

desc:
The ground suddenly falls away to the north. The birch trees here are giving the
edge of the cliff a wide berth. A few tree roots reach out and over the edge of
the cliff, helplessly dangling in the air. Grass grows up to the edge of the
cliff, providing some much needed traction. The ground slopes down from the
cliff to the south.
name: Cliff in the Forest

desc:
A group of birch trees is growing at the top of a long cliff. The trees are
growing right up to the edge of the cliff and some look like they might fall
off. The cliff is covered in roots and grasses, providing some traction. A
portion of the cliff extends out about thirty feet from the base. The ground
slopes away from the cliff to the south.
name: Cliff in the Forest

desc:
This area is very dim. The forest canopy has almost completely sealed off the
outside light. Short grass grows in the darkness provided by the trees branches.
Dead leaves cover the trees, while a few have what looks like a beard of lichen.
The top of a cliff lies to the east, while the ground slopes downwards gradually
to the south.
name: A Thicket of Birch Trees

desc:
A very large birch tree stands here, surrounded by smaller birches. The smaller
birches are standing around the larger tree, appearing to guard it from the
elements. A few flowers and ferns grow around the base of the large birch. There
are a few breaks in the canopy, allowing light to stream in. Trees await in all
directions.
name: At a Large Birch Tree

desc:
The birch trees here are very spread out. The ground is covered in a short,
green grass. Some ferns have popped up in patches around the few stones that
rest here. All passage to the west is blocked by a pile of boulders. A wall of
stone rises out of the ground, continuing to rise as it heads to the west. The
forest completely encompasses this area.
name: A Thicket of Birch

desc:
A vertical wall of stone rises to the north. The base is littered with large
rocks. In fact, a pile of boulders have stacked up against the wall, blocking
travel to the east. The face of the stone wall is very rough, but the masses of
moss clinging to it make the rock face impossible to climb. The wall slopes
quickly down to the east, appearing to end just beyond the pile of boulders.
name: The Forest Boundary

desc:
The birch trees are loosely dispersed throughout the area. They are covered with
dead leaves and a few have beards of lichen. Green grass is growing around the
masses of tree roots, with a few ferns scattered around. The ground slopes up
sharply to the south.
name: Under the Birch Trees

desc:
Grasses grow this small plateau and there is a distinctive lack of trees. The
only other flora is a variety of ferns. The land is quite higher, being south of
a steep incline that leads to a dell just north. Southward the land makes a
sharp drop as a sheer rock face dotted with some mosses makes travel that
direction completely undesireable.
name: On a Plateau in the Forest

desc:
The birch trees here appear to be a bit taller than their neighbours. They, like
most of the other trees, are still clad in the leaves of yesteryear. Short,
green grasses grow around the base of the trees, and ferns grow up around the
exposed roots of the trees. There is a steep rock cliff to the south and the
ground slopes upwards to the east.
name: Below a Cliff in the Forest

desc:
A scattering of rowan trees grow between the tufts of short, green grass that
covers much of the ground. Some small ferns have sprouted around their roots,
just beginning their lives in contrast to the large trees that are covered in
dead leaves from seasons past. The ground rises gradually to the south. What
looks like the bottom of a cliff can be seen to the east.
name: Beneath the Rowans

desc:
This area rises about fifty feet above the rest of the forest floor. Small ferns
dot the patches of grass that cover the top and sides of the hill. The rowan
trees here aren't any shorter than the trees in the rest of the forest. The
stale air has also become thinner with the increase in altitude. All directions
lead back down to the forest floor.
name: A Hill in Fangorn

desc:
A few rocks are scattered around the landscape. This area is very dim, as there
are no breaks in the branches of the massive pine trees to let any light
through. Short grass grows all over the area and it seems to flourish in the
darkness provided by the trees. Dead leaves cover all of the pines, while a few
have what look like beards of lichen.
name: Under the Pines

desc:
A sharp ridge ascends to the west from the forest floor. The pine trees here are
covered in a massive amount of dead leaves. The air is stale and the visibility
has decreased under the forest canopy. The ground is covered in rocks and
smaller stones. A sheer drop awaits to the south while the forest unfolds to the
east.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
The steep slope ends abruptly in a sheer drop to the south. The surface of the
ridge has been beaten smooth by time. There is no plant life attached to the
slippery rock. The ridge disappears into the east, but flattens out to the west,
allowing a myriad of bushes to take over the bare rock.
name: Ascending Mountain Ridge

desc:
The mountain ridge is almost completely covered with green bushes. Although the
ridge is very high, all you can see of the ground below is trees. The ridge
continues on up to the west until it meets a peak of the Misty Mountains. The
mountain slopes down slowly to the north. A sheer drop waits to the south. The
ridge runs down into the east until it disappears into Fangorn.
name: High atop a Mountain Ridge

desc:
The area is completely devoid of plant life. Only large, grey rocks dot the
landscape. The rocks are large enough that they would provide a decent amount of
shade from the daytime sun. The slope is slight and it continues up to a ridge
farther to the south. A very steep climb can be had to the east.
name: Ascending Mountain

desc:
The mountain rises to a level area before starting to ascended very quickly. The
slope is so steep, it would take a master climber to be able to scale this rock
face. The rock face is bare and almost devoid of foot or hand holds. To the
west, the slope looks easier to climb. The ridge runs east into Fangorn.
name: Steeply Rising Mountain

desc:
The terrain rises gradually to the west. The ground is littered with stones and
a few clumps of ferns dot the surroundings. A large rock is here, and a few
holes in it would allow a skilled climber to climb to the top. The tall pine
trees have grown in a circle around the boulder.
name: The Forest Threshold

desc:
The forest floor is free of rocks and other debris. A warm, green grass covers
almost the entire area. Some sweet smelling ferns grow in patches under a few of
the pine trees. The trees are carrying the leaves of a generation, but one of
the pines seems to have more than the rest. To the west, a rather large rock
lies amongst the trees. The forest spreads out in all directions.
name: Inside the Forest

desc:
Something must have happened here. One of the great rowan trees has been ripped
from the ground and its trunk completely blocks off passage to the east. Dead
leaves and lichen litter the grass about the dead tree. The other trees seem to
have formed a circle around their fallen comrade, almost as if mourning. The
forest continues on in the other directions.
name: A Thicket of Rowan

desc:
Willow trees grow all around this area. They stand tall, almost regally,
untouched by the passage of time. The ground is covered with their roots, making
walking difficult. Some ferns and grasses grow in patches that have yet to be
disturbed by the tree roots. The ground slopes down to the west, but the roots
of the trees, along with the steepness of the incline, make it too difficult to
navigate.
name: A Group of Willow Trees

desc:
Green grass and some sweet-smelling ferns grow in the shade that the forest
provides. The trees growing here seem to encircle a much thicker tree, whose
massive roots have completely blocked off travel to the east. What looks like a
depression in the forest can be seen to the west.
name: A Track of Willow Trees

desc:
The ground is covered with masses of tree roots, only penetrated by the trunks
of the massive willow trees. The trees grow straight up into the sky, creating a
dense, almost impenetrable canopy with their branches. The roots have grown into
a wall, blocking travel to the east.
name: Beneath the Willows

desc:
The oak trees here are covered in dead leaves. The roots of the trees twist in
and out of the ground, mingling with each other above ground before returning
underground. Green grass grows in between the masses of tree roots, with a few
ferns sticking up. The ground dips slightly to the south, into what looks like a
small valley. The forest extends in all other directions.
name: Entrance to a Dell

desc:
The oak trees here are very broad across their trunks. These trees are some of
the thickest in the whole forest. They have beards of lichen and wear their dead
leaves like heavy winter coats. Green grass covers the floor of the forest. A
single break in the canopy above yields a bit of light. The forest extends in
all directions.
name: A Group of Oaks

desc:
The oak trees grow here in what appears to be a circular pattern. One large oak
tree grows in the centre. The ground is covered with short, green grass and some
sweet-smelling ferns are growing between the tangles of the tree roots. Through
the trees, what appears to be a small hill can be seen to the west.
name: A Circle of Oaks

desc:
The forest opens into a knoll of wild grasses and ferns. There is one massive
oak tree at the top of the knoll, seemingly surveying the rest of the forest. At
the base of the knoll, a small burrow entrance has been dug. The forest
encompasses this area.
name: A Knoll in the Forest

desc:
Some stones have been deposited here by an ancient rockslide. Grass grows around
the rocks, with some sweet smelling ferns poking out. The pine trees in this
area are remarkably free of dead leaves and lichen. The air is a bit stuffy,
making it slightly difficult to breathe at first. The forest stretches out in
all directions.
name: A Group of Pines

desc:
The rocky terrain is flat and level. The trees seem to be more concentrated here
than in the other areas of the forest. The pines are wearing beards of lichen
and the dead leaves are reminiscent of the fur coat on a mammal. Small ferns dot
the landscape. The base of a mountain rises out of the forest to the north.
Trees stand in all other directions.
name: Amidst the Pine Trees

desc:
The terrain slopes upwards to the south. The pine trees have grown around a
rather large boulder that has rolled down off of the mountain. The trees stifle
the air here, making it hard to breath. To the west, the mountain rises up into
the clouds while more of Fangorn waits to the east.
name: The Edge of the Forest

desc:
The side of the mountain is covered in thick green brush. Many rocks are
scattered about, ranging in size from that of a petite hobbit to a massive
troll. The mountain continues to rise steadily to the south. The mountain ends
in a wide ridge farther south. The edge of a dark forest lies to the east.
name: Rising Mountain

desc:
The base of the mountain is covered by green shrubs. The mountain slopes up to
the south at a very leisurely pace, an easy climb for anyone. Another mountain
rises to the north. To the east, a group of tall dark trees have filled the
valley created by the feet of one of the peaks of the Misty Mountains.
name: Base of a Mountain

desc:
Multiple yellow sandstone boulders ply their way out of the mountainside here.
Smooth, but shaped at odd angles, these rocks reach out from the dark brown
earth and flow past the yellow-green grass blades that line the mountain. Among
the soft foliage of the mountain lie a few other boulders that still are
implanted in the rock.
name: Mountainside

desc:
In the distance the mountains still climb, but at a more drastic and chaotic
rate than before. Grey chunks of rock litter the ground in forms of oddly shaped
pebbles and boulders, between the debris grow a few thin yellow-gray coloured
grasses in stark contrast to the landscape in the east where a grey forest can
be spotted.
name: Side of the Mountain

desc:
The ridge here ends at the base of the mountains. The wind screams down from the
cold white peaks bearing a biting cold chill. To the south, the ridge continues
its way downwards as a brown streak of broken rock and yellow grass. To the far
east the ground is silhouetted by an ancient forest, grey and ominous.
name: Elevated Ridge

desc:
The ground slopes slowly upward and erodes away into a jagged structure of solid
rock. Following the lines of the ridge, the mountains ascend once again at a
renewed pace. At lower elevations the vegetation thrives and then booms into a
grey forest. At the top of the rocky slope lies a cluster of stones that denote
the entrance to a dim moist cave descending deep into the earth.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
A thin spine of grey rock pierces its way out of the crude ground. Pebbles and
sharp rocks layer the landscape. In the distance towards the mountains, the
spine continues, while to the east and west the land curves upwards. Towards the
far east a grey forest can be spotted, covered in mist.
name: Mountain Ridge

desc:
The terrain slopes downward from the north with deep scrapes in the ground due
to wind and rain. Grey rocks poke out at all angles giving a bit of shelter to
the occasional patches of yellow grass. Very little plant life has managed to
find enough soil to take root within the crags of this forsaken ridge.
name: Rockslide

desc:
Grey rocks cover the ground, allowing very little grass to grow. Short thin
blades of yellow grass grow in small patches among the rocks and boulders. A
large amount of rubble crawls out from the ground in the rugged northern ridge
and then roughly proceedes towards the rockslide that sits in the south.
name: Rocky Plateau

desc:
Brown pebbles litter the ground. Their curved surfaces are nearly smooth from
weathering. Between them spawn many small grasses, yellow in colour and small
among the pebbles and moss. A few larger rocks accumulate to the west. Farther
to the south and east the ground becomes more malleable until it reaches a wall
of trees and roots.
name: Mountains

desc:
Trees can be seen digging their way into the earth in every direction except to
the north. Their gnarled roots crawl across the earth, splicing groves in which
grasses grow and pebbles are planted. Looking farther up, the roots become a
giant wall of trunks and branches, stretching upwards and topped with a canopy
of immense size.
name: Forest Perimeter

desc:
Thick knotted grey roots sprawl across the ground. Around them small ferns grow
and upon them, mosses in return. The stale air is very misty, stringy lichen
hang from branches and trunks. Everywhere, spring trunks from a pile of roots
and they stretch into the sky with branches sticking out in all directions. A
thick canopy of branches and leaves makes it difficult to tell how high the
trees reach by glancing upward.
name: Fangorn Forest

desc:
A jungle of weeping willows grows here, each having its branches reach high and
then bedeck the area with its curtains of tear like leaves. As the mist wisps in
a ghostlike apparition through the wood, one cannot help but notice the wet
ground, littered with pebbles and vegetation of small ferns and light grasses.
name: Grove of Willows

desc:
Oak trees grow loosely around the area, creating plenty of room for the growth
of ferns and grasses. The roots of the trees poke up out of the ground in
places, creating many good resting spots. The trees begin to fan out to the
south, creating what looks like the entrance to a secluded dell. The trees
continue on in all other directions.
name: Oaken Forest

desc:
Very large oak trees grow here. They are covered in dead leaves that have never
fallen. The ground is covered in a short green grass. Sweet-smelling ferns grow
beneath some of the trees, creating a distinct fragrance. The canopy of the
forest is pierced by only the slightest amount of outside light, making this
area very dim.
name: Inside the Forest

desc:
Many large oak trees stand here in silence, unwavering in the dank stagnant air
that has been trapped by their massive branches. They are covered with dead
leaves and lichen. The rocks that have fallen on the land just west of here from
the mountains in that same direction have left this area untouched seemingly
just out of their reach. The forest extends in all directions.
name: Amidst the Oaks

desc:
There are fewer rocks here than along the border of the forest. Some green ferns
have sprouted on and around the roots of some of the larger pine trees that
dominate this area. The ferns and roots look like hairs covering pairs of large
flat feet while the trees look of old men, with beards of lichen and dead
leaves. All around, more of the vast forest awaits.
name: Inside the Forest

desc:
The ground is covered in rocks and stones from rockslides coming down the high
slopes of the Misty Mountains. Tall pine trees have grown about the slope, not
minding the rocks. Some soft grass has sprouted up around the bottoms of the
rocks enjoying the shade. An endless amount of trees extends to the east and
south.
name: Western Border of the Entwood

desc:
The ground slopes upward to the north where it joins the rest of the mountain.
The area is covered with green shrubs and many large rocks rest in the lower
areas. A valley of pine trees lies to the west. Fangorn awaits to the south,
while the mountain beckons from the north.
name: Base of a Mountain

desc:
The rocky terrain coming off the base of mountains has formed a small valley.
Pine trees have grown in the natural shield created by the mountain. The trees
are covered in the needles of years long past. The air inside the valley is very
stuffy and hard to breathe. The forest opens up to the south.
name: A Valley between the Feet of a Mountain

desc:
Many fragments of what once were great rocks together with mounds of disturbed
earth lie in a catastrophic disarray. Within the now shattered grey bedrock lie
deep gouges, and within them streaks of rust and silver veins of iron. To the
southeast of this collapsed mountainside lies a seemingly very thick and grey
forest. The ridge rises gently further to the north.
name: Rubble-strewn Ridge

desc:
The earth here consists of a rich, dark red flavour, possibly due to iron in the
bedrock. Green grasses grow with great vigour upon this red ridge, and they sway
calmly with the wind in waves of great beauty. Among them are also many clusters
of rocks leaving the impression of an ore field. Yet among the grey mountains,
the green that towers in the southeast is easily spotted, for a giant forest
springs from within that area.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
Flowing downwards, the grim brown mountains follow the same smooth circular
contour that can be seen for miles. Many short yellow grasses thrive in patches
among the grey rocks that spot the earth, while larger grey sandstone boulders
lie buried in the ground. To the southeast lies a misty forest of great size
from which a warm current of air rises upwards.
name: Mountainside

desc:
Sloping into the ground, the valley is drastically different than the nearby
environment. Grasses grow much taller, reaching nearly twice the height of the
higher altitude strains. Large stones reveal themselves among the grasses, in
rather unexpected places. A thick, grey forest of trees lies to the far east.
name: Dip into a Valley

desc:
A giant slab of grey rock sits implanted into the earth. Its multilayered form
is coloured in many intricate variations of grey and then slowly turns to a
green as more dirt and moss cling to its base. Around it, there grow multiple
types of grasses, some long and some short, but none as tall as the immense grey
wall of ancient tree trunks that blockades the south.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Small brown pebbles litter the ground in random patterns. Locked around them
grow yellow-green grasses and ferns. As the forest approaches from the south the
rocks start to accumulate in grey piles, from which the forest mist glides over
onto the ground. A wall of predominantly oak and other species of trees grow
here and to the south, blocking the view.
name: Forest Edge

desc:
A grey canopy lies above, interwoven with branches and twigs, blocking out most
of the light that tries to pierce its way through. The slope of the ground rises
noticeably to the north, where a giant limestone boulder lies covered in roots
and fallen leaves. The air is stuffy and covered in a grey mist.
name: Misty Forest

desc:
The forest flows into this small dell. The floor of the dell is covered with
green grass and ferns. The oak trees here have fewer leaves, but more lichen on
their trunks than in other areas of the forest. The roots of the trees,
combining with the steepness of the inclines, make travelling any way other than
north impossible.
name: A Dell in the Forest

desc:
The oak trees growing here are surrounded by their massive roots. A very small
amount of grasses and a few ferns are able to grow in this choked atmosphere.
The ground slopes down to the east, into what looks like a dell. Unfortunately,
the ground is too steep and the roots do not provide enough footholds to be able
to climb down.
name: Under the Oaks

desc:
The tall rowan trees are growing around an old, dead log. Dead leaves and lichen
litter the ground around the log, suggesting it has not been lying there too
long. Green grasses grow all around the area and some ferns are sprouting around
the exposed roots of the trees. The log completely blocks off travel to the
west.
name: Beside the Log

desc:
A smooth overhang projects itself across the cliff, providing shelter to a small
spring. The water trickles down from the side of the cliff into a crystal clear
collecting pond. Green mosses grow nearby in the comfort of the scattered grey
pebbles that make up the floor. The top of a ridge is seen in the east, and the
ground slopes down in the south to what appears to be a mountain pass.
name: Overhang in the Cliff

desc:
Here a huge barren rock sits a foot higher than the rest of the ridge and lies
implanted in the ground. Around it protrude other rocks from the ground,
coloured in grey and brown similar to those found at lower heights. The ground
slowly climbs towards the grey mountains, making very slow progress. In the
distance, at the bottom of the mountains in the east, a grey and shrouded forest
can barely be seen.
name: Broken Ridgetop

desc:
Rocks of multicoloured grey, brown, and red stand out in jagged formations in
the land. The barren wasteland of stone is not very stable and rocks constantly
tip if too much pressure is applied by anyone that treads upon them. A rocky
cliff stands to the north, leading from east to west. From where the cliff heads
to the distant mountains, the ground slopes eastward into a grassy plateau and a
sombre wood.
name: Mountain Canyon

desc:
In lines of green, yellow, and blue, the field upon the plateau waves as the
mountain air blows down across grass blades in a coquettish dance. Jutting
through the soil are sharp grey rocks that lead toward the north and west where
they are met by a giant cliff. Leading down from the heights, the land drops
into a rock covered terrain that abruptly halts at the eve of a forest.
name: Grassy Mountainside

desc:
Grasses of vibrant greens and yellows grow around the plateau. Everything sways
in the wind with the exception of a giant boulder that sits permanently in the
soil. The dark cliff that has been trailing in the north now ends abruptly and
turns towards the west. Similarly, the shrouded forest in the southwest follows
the same contour.
name: Boulder in the Plateau

desc:
Grasses in a multitude of greens and yellows billow in waves across the land,
moving according to the wind that blows down from the mountains. All around
these wild grasses grow, hardy from winter months and frigid air. A cliff blocks
the way westward and stretches from the white mountains all the way down to the
warmer lowlands. To the east, a dim forest stands, covered in mist.
name: Grassy Plateau

desc:
A crumbling spine of scattered brown rocks crawls up the ridge. Some grasses
grow in tufts and alter in colour as the land rises. To the west the rocks
stretch off towards the seemingly unreachable snowy mountains beyond the cliff.
A grey forest topped in dark green grows to the north.
name: Rising Ridge

desc:
Different sized slabs of dark rocks pile at the bottom of the cliff. Vertical
grooves and overhangs are scratched into the sheer sides that stretch from west
to east. The top of the cliff is barely visible as an abrupt halt of broken
rocks. The base sprawls across the landscape and is met by a misty forest in the
northeast.
name: Towering Cliffside

desc:
Fallen debris lies scattered all about the ground, creating a maze of rock
coming out of the cliff wall. The gouged rock on the cliff glistens with water,
only enough to make the cliff damp. A misty forest lines the cliff to the north
and east, nearly reaching the same height as the wall of stone. A chilly breeze
blows down from the cliff heights.
name: Beneath a Cliff

desc:
A dark cliff rises to the south, littering the ground with slabs of dark rock.
It is gouged with vertical and horizontal grooves, places from where the slabs
might have fallen. Away from the cliff, in the distance, a forest can be seen
shrouded by mist, reaching an immense height.
name: Beneath a Cliff

desc:
Grey roots crisscross on the ground, shattering the earth with their strength.
They grow around each other and smother almost everything, leaving barely enough
space for smaller plant life, such as ferns and mosses that grow in cracks. The
trees themselves reach an enormous height and create a thick canopy that allows
no light to pierce it.
name: Fangorn Forest

desc:
Grey trees tower around this area, while little grasses pop out of the ground.
Dull coloured ferns thrive here and their small leaves cover the forest floor.
The trees tower above the rest of the plant life, their branches and leaves
entangled, blocking out the light. The moist ground dips and rises, leaving
holes underneath roots where a few mud-stained mushrooms slowly grow.
name: Grey Forest

desc:
The ground is almost absent of plant life, a few scraggly grasses manage to take
root and many sun-tanned sandstone rocks are displayed in low lying curved
packets. With the exception of the west and north, there lies a forest of great
trees, their branches reaching enormous heights and their gnarled trunks forming
a wall. As the slope continues westward, the grasses grow in great quantities.
name: Mountains

desc:
Sun bleached rocks are scattered throughout the dirt here. The earth is hard,
being mostly clay, and the signs of erosion are very visible. Ruts are scrawled
into the earth with the exception of a few spots of solid sandstone that endure
the onslaught. To the north a grey forest is seen, and in the distance the
mountain seems to reach its highest peak.
