name: Sheer Mountainside

desc:
The earth begins to slope to the south and to climb to the north, all in harmony
with the dull dirt-covered mountains to the west. Here, the forest is a bit more
colourful. Huge boles and strong roots start at the base and climb upward into
the canopy of green leaves. Further within the forest, a mist can be seen
floating about.
name: Forest Boundary

desc:
To the northeast, there lies the Forest of Fangorn. A wall of grey brown tree
trunks stops all further sight deeper into the forest. Their branches tower
above at a terrible height, creating a huge curtain of twigs and leaves that
blocks out any light from above. To the southwest lie the roots of the
snow-capped mountains.
name: Forest Perimeter

desc:
Paper bark litters the ground where some magnificent birch trees have shed their
skins. Brittle orange leaves grow on their branches, and upon the ground a grey
and orange blanket has been lain over time. High above in the canopy, the birch
joins the storm of leaves and branches that helps the forest keep the light from
above at bay, and the forest grey and dim.
name: Grove of Birch

desc:
A great wall of trunks, roots, and branches lie directly to the east. It is an
ominous visage as the trees reach up and blanket the sky. The ground is moist,
harbouring many small green grasses and reddish stones that seem to have crawled
down from the mountains. A grey mist seems to flow in from the east, the most
likely source of the moisture found on the ground.
name: Forest Border Line

desc:
This is a very small, cramped burrow. The inside of the walls are covered with
blood and small bones litter the ground around the entrance. A mat of grass and
fur lies in a corner. A shallow indentation has been dug into the back wall.
name: Fox Lair

desc:
This rather large cave has been carved into the soft dirt. Fur and feathers
cover a section of the ground, providing a nice place for the cave's inhabitants
to rest. Bones and bits of flesh are strewn around the ground. Towards the back
of the den small tunnels run deeper into the ground.
name: Wolves Den

desc:
The top of the boulder is largely flat. It is covered in moss and ferns that
have begun to grow in the small cracks and crevices scattered atop the boulder.
All that can be seen past the edge of the boulder is the trunks of the massive
pine trees that grow in this area.
name: On Top of a Large Boulder

desc:
In the centre of the watchtower's thick support beams lies a large, smoking
fire. An ornate chair sits close to the fire, surrounded in a pool of blood. The
bones of many creatures lie on the floor, covered in piles of soot and other
debris. Directly in front of the foremost tower support is the entrance to what
looks like a cave. A rickety ladder runs up to the top of the tower.
name: Beneath the Tower

desc:
The top of the tower is a cramped lookout. The structure is enclosed with
loosely nailed slabs of wood. A railing surrounds the edge, preventing anyone
from falling off. The only furnishing is a chair sitting in the northwestern
corner. A creaky wooden ladder leads back down into the camp.
name: Orkish Lookout

desc:
This shallow cave was delved using only the hands of the miners. Long, deep
scratches run the length of each wall, a testament to the methods of the orcs.
Shelves have been attached to the walls to hold any necessary tools. Broken
tools litter the floor, along with the bones of uncooperative miners. An old,
wooden door is seated to the north, marking the entrance to the mines.
name: Supply Room

desc:
The black stones that line the cave have claw marks riddling their sides. The
cave reaches upwards in a ragged arc and then sweeps downward to the north in a
more constricted area. A deep layer of muck lies on the ground and is pebbled
with broken bones and tools. A line of metal mine cart rails has been lain here
and they move towards the north and south where a wooden door has been installed
to keep things in, not out. Crude wooden torches line the walls.
name: Exit from the Mines

desc:
The tunnel has dimmed considerably, a very little amount of light from the
torches arrives from the northern and southern parts of the mines, but is enough
to cast a few shadows across the matted floor. Claw marks riddle the darkened
rock that plates the walls in jagged formations. A cold draft of air flows in
from the south and moves northwards along the iron rails and broken tools.
name: Dim Mines

desc:
Once again the torches have been driven into the sides of the tunnel, their
light sending dancing shadows across the tunnel. Among the debris that covers
the ground one can make out several small fragments of iron ore. A pair of
rusted metal rails move from the south to the north, following the cold draft of
air that is in the orkish mine of the Urughash.
name: Mines

desc:
The tunnel walls of black rock gleam in the light that flickers from the bonfire
in the north. From the north lead a pair of rusted rails that move along
southward in the trampled ground. Strange shadows jeer about as the lights from
torches in the south reach this section of the tunnel. Among the debris of
broken tools and bones a cold draft of air can be felt.
name: Mines

desc:
The tunnel here has been enlarged greatly, the cave walls heavily marked with
scratches and many stone slabs serving as seats in the centre where a campfire
blazes. The cavern floor has been pressed down from all the tramping of many
feet and it is heavily littered with disarrayed piles of broken cups and other
rubbish. The area appears to have been mined out leaving nothing left. A
collection of tree trunks and branches provide ample fuel for the fire.
name: Enlarged Tunnel

desc:
The light glows brightly to the south where tongues of a bonfire can be seen,
sending shadows down the tunnels in sombre waves. Dark rock has been picked,
chiselled, and broken from the solid rock walls, making progress deeper into the
mines for the orcs. A metal line of rusted rails make their way from the north
to the south among the many broken tools and rocks that litter the ground.
name: Mines

desc:
At this section of the mine a multitude of torches have been set up, their glow
casting eerie shadows upon the rocks that scatter upon the wet roughened walls.
A deep hole has been cut into the western side of the mine where the work has
doubled. The gleam of iron ore explains the extra mining that has taken place.
An old set of rusted rails has been laid here, running from the north to the
south, among the patted down earth and rubble.
name: Lit Mine

desc:
Following the rusted set of mine cart rails, the sides of the roughened tunnel
slowly start to narrow at the middle. Shadows appear an interesting game as the
flickering torchlight reaches this section of the tunnel from afar. Dark veins
of ore spread along the sides of the wall towards the north following the cold
draft that can be felt in the dusty air of the orkish tunnels.
name: Mines

desc:
The orkish tunnel has entirely collapsed here. Large cracks run down the sides
of the tunnel where it still holds, hinting towards further danger if a lot of
movement were to occur. A large pile of rubble and dirt lies at the far east
side of the tunnel, their shadows flickering in the dim torchlight. The intact
portion of the tunnel leads onwards to the west, where more torchlight can be
seen.
name: Dangerous Cave-in

desc:
The cave expands until it reaches a small underground lake. On its shores lie
small black pebbles and a thick layer of muck. Most of the cave floor consists
of this dirt, but not nearly as wet. To the west the cave seems to have
collapsed and the only other passage is to the north, where all the air within
the cave seems to be drifting towards. The constant dripping of water into the
lake can be heard.
name: Cavern Well

desc:
Within the cavern lie dark slabs of solid black rock. The ceiling is mostly open
until the cave jaggedly progresses deeper into the earth among the slabs that
have already fallen from above. On the east side, near the exit, lies a natural
ladder of steps formed from the black rock that leads up into the mountains. A
draft of cold air rushes from the inside of the cave.
name: Sullen Cave

desc:
A pair of flickering torches mark the path where the mines take a bend to the
east and south. A hard concoction of rocks and mud covers the ground among an
old set of rusted iron rails that lead southwards. It seems the orcs were trying
to mine to the east, but dark cracks in the ceiling tell a more horrendous tale.
The ceiling consists of dark roughened rock, its sides grated with pick axe
marks, causing large crevices to appear within the rock.
name: Crude Bend in the Mine

desc:
This brick building is of fairly crude design. It is practical and sturdily
built though, able to withstand time and use. A large wooden door to the south
is the only exit, while stairs provide access to the top of tower. The interior
is furnished sparsely for the use of a few soldiers, with two beds in the
corner. It was built to watch the entrance to Nan Curunir, not to withstand a
siege, but merely to serve as a post.
name: Inside the Watchtower

desc:
The small tower watches over the entrance to the valley of Isengard. Peering out
from the arrow slits, would show why its builders chose this particular spot for
the watchtower. The mouth of the valley is a few miles across, and from this
viewpoint any intruders could be noticed.
name: Top Floor of the Watchtower

desc:
This medium-sized room seems to have been added on to the watchtower after the
main building was constructed, as the wood is significantly younger. Several of
the support beams have been placed throughout the room and along the walls and
in-between them, lanterns are hung around the room to light it during the dark
hours. A few chairs are thrown about the room carelessly, as if perhaps their
occupants had to leave in a hurry.
name: Meeting Room

desc:
This small room appears to have been sectioned off from the larger room to the
south, forming a small storage area. The wooden boards underfoot are neat and in
good condition. A few maps of Rohan and the surroundings have been placed
carefully along the eastern wall below a small window. The window itself has
been painted over and nailed shut to avoid unwanted visitors.
name: Storage Closet

desc:
The ground is suddenly met by a giant wall of roots and trees. Nothing else can
be seen past a few feet into the grey wood. Ferns and grasses grow along the
forest floor accompanied by moss on the trunks. Looking up, there is only a web
of grey branches and green leaves. The only thing in the far distance that can
be seen is the line of trees and the dark cliff that rises in the south.
name: Forest Rim

desc:
The grass in this area has been stomped to the ground. A camp lies to the west,
about one mile outside the forest border. It appears to be covered by a large
canopy. A trail of dried blood and tree bark leads into the camp. The camp is
situated so that the inhabitants have easy access to the trail to the south.
name: In Front of a Camp

desc:
Dried blood covers the ground around the entrance to the camp. A pair of skulls
spiked to the end of poles flank the hole leading out onto the trail. A heavily
stitched canopy hangs overhead, blocking out any natural light. The air is heavy
with soot and smells of unspeakable things. The camp opens to the west, where it
surrounds the base of a tower.
name: Inside the Camp

desc:
The ground above has collapsed, forming this dusty, rocky cave. Sharp rocks and
debris, partially hidden in the soft earth, are strewn all about. A pile of
larger rocks form a makeshift wall to the north. Small strands of dirt sift down
from cracks in the ceiling continuously, giving the illusion that something tiny
is digging in the sand, creating a miniscule but constant movement.
name: Dusty Crack

desc:
The dike is deep and does not seem to have eroded naturally. With even, smooth
walls the dike effectively prevents armies trying to cross, both from north and
south. The ground is uneven, with many sharp rocks and debris scattered. The
dike continues in both the east and west directions.
name: In a Deep Dike

desc:
The waterfall dominates the entire cave, with its constantly moving water as a
thick curtain to the south. The other walls are in contrast with their hard
black rock. Everything is covered with water; the humid air deposits drops
everywhere. The wall of water to the south whispers a never-ending murmur,
blocking out all sounds from the outside, making room for an unreal silence.
name: Wet Cave behind the Waterfall
